steps.explanation: Identify the rate. Juan rides at 5.7 minutes per mile, meaning each mile takes 5.7 minutes. Total time = rate × number of miles.
Write the function: \(m(x) = 5.7 \cdot x = 5.7x\). This is a direct proportionality with slope 5.7.
Check with \(x = 1\): \(m(1) = 5.7(1) = 5.7\) minutes for 1 mile — matches the given rate. Check \(x = 2\): \(m(2) = 11.4\) minutes for 2 miles, which is exactly double. Correct.

• Choice A: \(m(x) = \frac{x}{5.7}\) — divides miles by minutes/mile, giving miles²/minute — wrong units and wrong operation. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: \(m(x) = x + 5.7\) — adds the rate as a constant; for \(x = 2\) gives 7.7, not 11.4. Incorrect. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: \(m(x) = x - 5.7\) — subtracts the rate; gives negative time for \(x < 5.7\). Nonsensical. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: \(m(x) = 5.7x\) — multiplies rate by miles to get total minutes. That matches choice D — D is correct.
